title: BWC & Incident Records: Victoria Rutan DUI Investigation (June 10, 2022)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.

I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) footage and the arrest report for the DUI investigation involving Victoria Rutan.

Specific details to assist your search:

Incident Date: June 10, 2022

Location: Volusia County, FL

Subject Name: Victoria Rutan

Incident Summary: Ms. Rutan was stopped under suspicion of DUI. During the interaction, she was highly argumentative, cited her profession as a nurse, and engaged in defensive  behavior toward the deputies before being taken into custody.

Records requested:

All BWC footage capturing the initial stop, Field Sobriety Tests (FSTs), and the custodial arrest.

The complete Arrest Affidavit and Narrative Report.

Any dispatch audio or bodycam footage from the transport to the Volusia County Jail.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Andrej Ilic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Sunset Cliffs Vehicle Rescue - Officer Jonathan Wiese (June 13, 2020)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.

I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) footage regarding the emergency rescue and arrest at Sunset Cliffs.

Specific details to assist your search:

Incident Date: June 13, 2020 (Approximately 5:00 AM)

Location: Sunset Cliffs Boulevard near Hill Street, San Diego, CA

Subject Name: Robert Brians

Incident Summary: A suspect intentionally drove a truck off a cliff into the ocean with his two-year-old twin daughters inside. Officer Jonathan Wiese utilized a 100-foot dog leash to rappel down the cliff face and rescue the children while detaining the suspect in the water.

Records requested:

All BWC footage from Officer Jonathan Wiese and other officers on the cliffside during the rescue and apprehension.

The full Arrest and Incident Report for suspect Robert Brians.

All 911 call recordings and dispatch audio relating to the initial welfare check and the crash.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrej Ilic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Juvenile Armed Standoff and Shootout (June 1, 2021)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.

I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the incident report, regarding the armed standoff involving two juvenile suspects.

Specific details to assist your search:

Incident Date: June 1, 2021

Location: 1000 block of Enterprise Osteen Road, Enterprise, FL

Suspect Names: Nicole Jackson-Maldonado (14) and Travis O’Brien (12)

Incident Summary: Two juveniles fled a group home, broke into a residence, and armed themselves with a shotgun and an AK-47. They engaged in a 30-minute shootout with Volusia County deputies. The footage is notable for the extreme restraint shown by deputies before being forced to return fire.

Records requested:

All BWC footage from the responding deputies capturing the negotiations and the final exchange of gunfire.

The full Narrative/Incident Report (Case Number 21-10515).

All 911 calls from the homeowners and dispatch audio during the 30-minute engagement.

Any aerial/drone footage if utilized during the perimeter.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Andrej Ilic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Lakewood Spree Shooting - Agent Ashley Ferris (December 27, 2021)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.

I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the shooting of spree shooter Lyndon McLeod by Agent Ashley Ferris.

Specific details to assist your search:

Incident Date: December 27, 2021

Location: South Vance Street and West Alaska Drive (Belmar area), Lakewood, CO

Suspect Name: Lyndon McLeod (alias Roman McClay)

Incident Summary: After a multi-city shooting spree, the suspect entered the Belmar shopping district. Agent Ashley Ferris confronted the suspect alone. The suspect shot Agent Ferris in the abdomen; despite her life-threatening injury, she returned fire and neutralized the suspect, ending the threat to the public.

Records requested:

All BWC and Dashcam footage from Agent Ashley Ferris and all other responding Lakewood officers involved in the final engagement and perimeter.

The complete Narrative/Incident Report, including the internal review of the officer-involved shooting.

All 911 emergency call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to the active shooter report and the "officer down" call.

Any surveillance camera footage from the Belmar shopping district/Hyatt House recovered by investigators.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrej Ilic

title: Community Oriented Policing Services (COPS) grants awarded to the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD) (Department of Justice, Community Oriented Policing Services (COPS))

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, 5 U.S.C. § 552, I am requesting records relating to Community Oriented Policing Services (COPS) grants awarded to the Los Angeles Police Department (LAPD), City of Los Angeles, California, for the period January 1, 1994 through December 31, 1997.

Specifically, I request:

1/ All grant award documents, grant agreements, and cooperative agreements between the COPS Office and the Los Angeles Police Department or the City of Los Angeles executed between January 1, 1994 and December 31, 1997, including but not limited to grants awarded under the COPS Ahead, COPS FAST, COPS MORE, and Universal Hiring Program (UHP) grant programs.

2/ All documents stating the number of officer positions funded by each grant award to the LAPD during this period, including any documentation of the total authorized officer positions and the grant's contribution to those positions.

3/ All documents stating the conditions, matching requirements, maintenance-of-effort obligations, and community policing implementation requirements attached to each grant award to the LAPD during this period.

4/ Any progress reports, compliance reports, or monitoring reports submitted by the LAPD or the City of Los Angeles to the COPS Office pursuant to any grant award during this period.

5/ Any correspondence between the COPS Office and the LAPD or the City of Los Angeles regarding grant conditions, compliance, or officer hiring targets during this period.

I am a journalist and author researching a book about the 1997 North Hollywood bank shootout and its institutional context. I request a fee waiver on the grounds that disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations and activities of the federal government, specifically the implementation of the 1994 Violent Crime Control and Law Enforcement Act's officer-hiring mandate and its effect on a major American police department. The information is not sought for commercial use.

If a fee waiver is not granted, please notify me before incurring costs in excess of $25.

If any portion of this request is denied, please release all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions and provide a written explanation of the basis for any withholding, citing the specific FOIA exemption(s) claimed.

I am available to clarify or narrow this request if it would assist processing. Please confirm receipt.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Daniel Brown

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Colorado Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.

I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the officer-involved shooting of Christian Glass.

Specific details to assist your search:

Incident Date: June 10, 2022

Location: Near Silver Plume, CO

Subject Name: Christian Glass

Incident Summary: A motorist called 911 for roadside assistance. Following a prolonged standoff with multiple agencies, an officer-involved shooting occurred while the suspect was inside his vehicle.

Records requested:

All BWC footage from all responding agencies (Clear Creek County, Idaho Springs PD, Colorado State Patrol) capturing the negotiation and the shooting.

The full Narrative/Incident Report.

The original 911 call placed by Christian Glass and all subsequent dispatch audio.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrej Ilic

Incident Date: March 31, 2018

Subject Name: Caryl Phillips (formerly Caryl Sue Phillips)

Incident Summary: A traffic stop was initiated on a vehicle containing acquaintances of Ms. Phillips. She arrived on the scene and engaged in a prolonged interaction with officers, utilizing her professional title in an attempt to influence the stop.

Records requested:

All BWC and Dashcam footage from the responding officers during the entirety of the interaction with Ms. Phillips.
